hard_constraints.continuity_rule: exact same mechanism state before resume

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg6_plus_l11_bestmean
candidate_specs.rationale: Current best-mean two-anchor reference: keep the strong anchor10 negative z pair and the current best-mean late11 zquad motif.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg4_plus_l11_maxbest
candidate_specs.rationale: Current cleanest saved compromise point inside the old frontier.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg6_plus_l11_maxbest
candidate_specs.rationale: Direct missing bridge between the current best-mean endpoint and the max-oriented late11 sibling: keep the stronger anchor10 neg6 z-pair, but swap the late11 block to maxbest.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg5_plus_l11_maxbest
candidate_specs.rationale: Midpoint bridge inside the anchor10 z-pair dwell between the saved neg6 and neg4 branches, still using the late11 maxbest motif.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg5_plus_l11_bestmean
candidate_specs.rationale: Midpoint refinement of the current best-mean family: same late11 best-mean motif, but anchor10 neg-z dwell softened from 6 s to 5 s.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg6_plus_l11_y9x1
candidate_specs.rationale: Keep the current anchor10 neg6 z-pair, but locally soften the late11 zquad from y10/x2 to the y9/x1 split.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg6_plus_l11_ypos10x1yneg8x1
candidate_specs.rationale: Keep the current anchor10 neg6 z-pair, but use the late11 asymmetric positive/negative-Y split that slightly softens the return-side Y exposure.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg6_plus_l11_y10x0back2
candidate_specs.rationale: Keep the current anchor10 neg6 z-pair, but move the late11 x-buffer fully to the resume side to test whether resume-side protection can push max down without giving back too much mean.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_ypair_neg2_plus_l11_maxbest
candidate_specs.rationale: Milder anchor10 y-pair test: halve the saved neg4 y dwell while keeping the late11 maxbest motif, to see whether dKg_zz / Ka2_y gains survive with less mean damage.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg4_then_ypair_neg2_plus_l11_maxbest
candidate_specs.rationale: Residual-focused hybrid at anchor10: first keep the useful neg4 z-pair scaffold, then add a mild neg2 y-pair before the late11 maxbest block. Intended to attack Ka2_y / dKa_yy / dKg_zz together while keeping Ka2_z protected.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_ypair_neg2_then_zpair_neg4_plus_l11_maxbest
candidate_specs.rationale: Same anchor10 hybrid ingredients as the previous candidate, but reverse the local order to see whether ending anchor10 with the z-pair better protects the already-good xz-family channels.

bottom_line.verdict: small but real interior-frontier progress: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg6_plus_l11_y10x0back2 gives back only 0.140 mean-points versus the current best-mean path while shaving 0.031 max-points. However the Ka2_y ceiling is still far above the current best-max endpoint (99.587) and nowhere near default18 (14.713).

scientific_conclusion: The residual-focused two-anchor push did uncover one extra non-dominated interior point (twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg6_plus_l11_y10x0back2) and one genuinely informative anchor10 hybrid (twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg4_then_ypair_neg2_plus_l11_maxbest). But the interior point only trades about 0.140 mean-points for 0.031 max-points, while the hybrid cuts dKa_yy / dKg_zz / Ka2_y much more aggressively only by paying about 0.746 mean-points. So this local family can still be bent, but not enough to produce a clear new unified winner: Ka2_y remains the stubborn ceiling, and the current saved endpoints still define the main branch decision.
